The raising qf the Banner is auspicious if it is neither too slow,
nor too fast, not shaky, and if its garlands, decorations and orna-
ments are not spoiled. If it is otherwise, it forebodes evil, and the
royal preceptor should mitigate it through expiatory ceremonies.

The wise opine that there is great danger in store for the king
if a carnivorous bird, owl, dove, crow or vulture sits on the Banner.
If it be a blue jay, it is dangerous to the Yuvaraja (prince); an eagle
